alerts.title: Iran nuclear breakout window narrows to 1-2 weeks amid stalled IAEA access
alerts.summary: Iran's uranium enrichment at 60% U-235 has compressed estimated breakout time to approximately 1-2 weeks, per global_metrics nuclear data. UN nuclear chief signals inspectors will visit Iranian sites, but Iran conditions access on completion of final deal. IAEA verification headlines dominate feed (NPR, South Carolina Public Radio, Foundation for Defense of Democracies), indicating urgent diplomatic pressure. Parallel cyber attacks on global infrastructure attributed to Iranian actors (Middle East Eye, Radio Free Europe) suggest regime preparing contingencies.

alerts.title: Iran strikes continue undermining Trump Hormuz agreement; UN evacuation paused
alerts.summary: IRGC Navy attack on cargo vessel 7.5 nautical miles off Oman coast (26.36°N, 56.1°E per incidents feed) persists as unresolved escalation. Multiple corroborating outlets (WSJ, Washington Post, CNN, PBS, NYT, BBC, CBS, Al Jazeera, Guardian) confirm Iranian attribution and vessel's unauthorized routing. UN agency formally paused evacuation operations through Strait of Hormuz. Middle East Spectator Telegram post (~48K views) confirms IRGC statement that vessel ignored Persian Gulf Strait Authority coordination and verbal warnings prior to strike.

alerts.title: US submarine USS Florida positioned in Persian Gulf as nuclear tension peaks
alerts.summary: USS Florida submarine now deployed in Persian Gulf (26.49°N, 52.22°E per assets feed) alongside existing Red Sea carrier concentration (USS Dwight D. Eisenhower, USS Bataan, destroyers Carney, Mason, Laboon). Multi-theater US presence includes USS Abraham Lincoln (Arabian Sea) and USS Gerald R. Ford (Mediterranean). Threat level remains CRITICAL (9/10). Submarine positioning signals readiness for undersea contingencies amid 1-2 week nuclear breakout estimate and Iranian maritime escalation.
alerts.region: Persian Gulf / Red Sea / Arabian Sea

alerts.title: Israel continues Lebanon strikes despite April ceasefire; 3 civilians killed
alerts.summary: Israeli airstrikes on southern Lebanon (33.75-34.01°N, 35.52-35.97°E per incidents feed) killed 3 civilians, including named targets, in violation of ceasefire framework that took effect in April 2026 (Middle East Eye, CBC, Al Jazeera, Axios). Hezbollah asserts ceasefire violation; pattern indicates Israel conducting unilateral operations outside diplomatic constraints. Concurrent Iranian Parliament Speaker and Chief Negotiator Qalibaf attendance at Tehran mourning ceremony (Telegram/ME Spectator, ~51K views) suggests regime positioning for potential retaliation response.

alerts.title: Houthis escalate Red Sea interdiction with formal ban on Israeli vessels
alerts.summary: Houthis announce blanket prohibition on Israeli shipping in Red Sea (15.83-16.16°N, 40.8-41.12°E per incidents feed), per Long War Journal and Asharq Al-Awsat reports. Multiple recent Houthi attacks on Israel documented (incidents category: naval). Declaration signals proxy escalation under Iranian direction alongside IRGC Strait of Hormuz strikes. Proxy force status elevated across board: Hezbollah (Active Exchanges), Iraqi Shiite Militias (Base Strikes Detected), Palestinian Islamic Jihad (Active) per global_metrics.

analysis_note: Iran nuclear breakout window has contracted to 1-2 weeks while IRGC simultaneously challenges Trump's Hormuz deal and proxies escalate across three theaters (Red Sea, Lebanon, Persian Gulf); US multi-carrier posture and submarine presence indicate preparation for imminent confrontation, but diplomatic verification mechanisms remain unresolved.
